steps.title: Spot the decisive cause
steps.content: The passage isolates one cause for the dialect's uniqueness: geographical isolation prevented exposure to language changes occurring elsewhere, allowing the dialect to retain older features. This causal relationship is decisive—it's why the dialect differs from others.

steps.title: Apply the cause to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C states that geographical isolation influences language development, which is exactly what the dialect's uniqueness demonstrates.
  • A focuses on self-protection from foreign influence (not addressed)
  • B presents language change as the conclusion rather than isolation's role
  • D discusses mutual intelligibility (unstated)
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
